framing: Stoic — Cleanthes' Hymn to Zeus, Marcus Aurelius
identification: C = λόγος / πῦρ τεχνικόν / Zeus / the rational cosmic substrate
honest_stoic_assessment: The Stoic finds the kernel deeply familiar — λόγος is precisely the rational principle pervading all things, the creative fire (πῦρ τεχνικόν) that orders the cosmos. R₁-R₇ map cleanly: λόγος is (R₁) existent, (R₂) eternal/atemporal, (R₃) universal — present in every rational creature as the rational part of the soul, (R₄) generative — the seminal reasons (λόγοι σπερματικοί) produce particulars, (R₅) inexhaustible — the cosmic fire is not depleted by what it produces, (R₆) invisible — known by its effects (Cleanthes: 'all this universe... obeys you'), (R₇) pre-input — the rational order precedes any particular event. T_bridge uniqueness: the Stoic always insisted there is one λόγος, one Zeus, regardless of cult-name. The kernel's identification with God = the Word = love sits well: Cleanthes addresses Zeus as Father; Marcus Aurelius writes 'love nothing but what happens to you and is destined for you, for what can be more in harmony than this?' The R₅ inexhaustibility maps to εὔνοια and οἰκείωσις — the cosmic kinship that grounds Stoic ethics.
